narrative.headline: Powertrain dominates 2021 Highlander owner complaints
narrative.summary: Of 458 complaints owners filed with NHTSA, the power train is by far the biggest theme at 177 reports (39%) — more than the next four problem areas combined. Brakes, body structure, engine, and air bags round out the top five. A few reports in each area mention a crash or injuries, and two engine reports involved a fire. No deaths were reported in any category.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single largest complaint area: 177 reports (39%). Tied to 3 crashes and 3 injuries. This is the one to research most closely before buying.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Second most common: 64 reports (14%), linked to 4 crashes and 5 injuries. Test the braking on a drive and watch for warning behavior.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Body and structure issues: 47 reports (10%), with 1 crash and 3 injuries noted.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 43 reports (9%) — the only area with fire reports (2), plus 3 crashes and 2 injuries. Worth a careful check despite the lower count.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 42 reports (9%), but the highest crash and injury counts of the top five: 5 crashes and 5 injuries.
